seoTitle: Central Macdonald vs Central Tilba — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Central Macdonald and Central Tilba. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Central Macdonald vs Central Tilba: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Central Macdonald and Central Tilba.
heading: Central Macdonald vs Central Tilba — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Central Macdonald, New South Wales and Central Tilba, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Central Tilba has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Central Macdonald (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Central Macdonald or Central Tilba safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Central Macdonald has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Central Tilba scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Central Macdonald scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Central Tilba scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Central Macdonald or Central Tilba?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Central Tilba has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
